notes: Observational autonomy. The eerie-warm chorus 'Waymo, Waymo, take me home' is the bed. The pivot is 'This is the most autonomous I will ever feel' — the thesis, half-ironic half-sincere. Ad-read comedy: 'Good thing I'm not...' trails off, the unspoken word is the joke and the truth. Outro 'You have arrived at your destination' has a 3.16s hold on 'at' — the AI voice dissolving.

chunks.note: Your driver is... no one — 2nd utterance, 1.62s hold on 'no' — the song's uncanny opening
Waymo, Waymo, take me home — first chorus pt1
Me and my prescriptions, riding alone — pt2
Waymo, Waymo, no one at the wheel — mixed = machine+human
This is the most autonomous I will ever feel — the thesis
Good thing I'm not — the trailed-off punchline, unspoken word is the joke
Waymo, Waymo, take me home — final chorus pt1
This is the most autonomous I will ever feel — final, biggest
You have arrived at your destination — 3.16s hold on 'at', the AI outro
